name: Tussoa
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 15,110

Once a humble fishing village, Tussoa has blossomed into a respectable city but still boasts a relaxed way of life.

Organizations: Humble temples are found near and around the Lotoe Plaza. Family businesses work beneath the marquis and the local guild masters.

name: Port Damali
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 82,110

As the unofficial capital of free trade outside the empire, Port Damali is a colorful confluence of cultures. Trade routes splinter from all sides of the city to ensure rapid transport of goods throughout Wildemount and beyond.

name: Palma Flora
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 1,780

A vacation destination for merchants from nearby ports, Palma Flora is famous for its annual shark hunting tournament.

name: Gwardan
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 18,900

Known for its sprawling mines of precious metals and stones, Gwardan is a cultural center for elves. The city is renowned for its smiths that use a unique style of metalworking called "steamcasting."

name: Vol'antim
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 3,890

The hidden cliffside sttlement of Vol'antim is nestled deep within the heart of the Cyrios Mountains. The community is home to aarakocra who live in relative seclusion.

name: Pride's Call
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 16,090

Within the rolling hills of the Zemni Fields, the city of Pride's Call became the center of hilldwarf culture in Wildemount after the Calamity.

name: Kamordah
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 7,440

Kamordah was built within the Bromkiln Hills by a collection of zealous followers of Desirat the Twilight Pheonix. Now the city boasts a carefully guarded industry for farming exotic herbs and fauna, made possible by geothermal activity.

name: Deastok
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 10,090

The sister city of Kamordah, Deastok is appears unusually affluent for its size due to the presence of the Truscan family.

name: Felderwin
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 8,180

As the heart of halfling society in Wynandir, Felderwin is surrounded by fertile farmland that has been cultivated by the halfling community for over seven centuries.

name: Grimgolir
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 19,090

The dwarven stronghold of Grimgolir sits at the core of an immense network of tunnels carved throughout the southern Dunrock Mountains. The tribulations of the city have shaped the ideology to which its dwarven citizens cling: to succeed is to struggle.

name: Hupperdook
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 12,090

The smoke-stacked city of Hupperdook is the heart of industry in the empire, manufacturing technological marvels and large-scale weapons of war for the Dwendalian Empire.

name: Bladegarden
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 9,910

Acting as both a training complex for the Righteous Brand and the core of orcish society in Western Wynandir, Bladegarden is a large military complex whose denizens are filled with a sense of honor and pride.

name: Ashguard Garrison
description:
Fortress · Wildemount · Population 5,270

A partner stronghold to the northern Rockguard Garrison, the Ashguard Garrison sits atop a steep mountain pass on the south side of the Brokenveil Bluffs.

name: Rockguard Garrison
description:
Fortress · Wildemount · Population 6,800

The Rockguard Garrison was constructed in a joint effort between the Crown and Grimgolir as a symbol of politcal union. The fortress has held strong for generations without being breached.

name: Urzin
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 5,930

A uniquely mobile settlement with its permanent dwellings built on the backs of dozens of horizonback tortoises, Urzin is home to swamp hunters, scouts, and trappers that know the Brokenveil Marsh like no one else.

name: Jigow
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 13,210

By negotiations sparked by the Kryn Dynasty, the coastal city of Jigow was formed from competing orc and goblin clans. It rose to become an important source for fishing commerce for the dynasty.

name: Bazzoxan
description:
Town · Wildemount · Population 2,610

Bazzoxan is perpetually locked in a stalemate between the Kryn Dynasty forces and the abyssal incursion from within the ruins of a temple to the Betrayer Gods.

name: New Haxon
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 1,250

The stalwart outpost of New Haxon is garrisoned by agents of the Cerberus Assembly, along with a contingent of allied mercenaries from nearby Draconia.

name: Rotthold
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 8,800

The lawless harbor city of Rotthold has no official governing body. Many outlanders view the crime-ridden settlement as little more than a collection of degenerate slums.

name: Igrathad
description:
The Many Hosts · Wildemount · Population 6,750

Once a battleground where regional factions feuded and warred over resources and glory, the Many Hosts of Igrathad is a scattered network of seven villages with robust communities of goblinkin, orcs and giants.

name: Charis
description:
Village · Wildemount · Population 950

The hidden hamlet of Charis was founded by an unlikely alliance between halflings and dragonborn. The village takes no value in gold or jewels, but instead, emphasize community and share equally.

name: Xarzith Kitril
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 9,110

Built shortly after the fall of Draconia, the fortified stone city of Xarzith Kitril continues to grow in population and industry.

name: Trostenwald
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 8,900

On the banks of the Ustaloch, the city of Trostenwald thrives on its booming brewing economy. The major brewing families hold a lot of power and are constantly competing with one another.

name: Alfield
description:
Town · Wildemount · Population 3,410

Supplemented by mines in the nearby hills, the small town of Alfield is often regarded as a stop on the route to more important business elsewhere.

name: Zadash
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 89,210

Once the capital of the Julous Dominion, Zadash sits at the central crossroads of the southern region of the empire. The city is a bastion of law, civilization, and military might in the Marrow Valley.

name: Berleben
description:
Town · Wildemount · Population 3,230

The marsh town of Berleben is nestled deep in the Labenda Swamp. Once promising economic boom in cultivated herbs, the town has floundered due to the instability of the marsh.

name: Druvenlode
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 12,110

Established in the early years following the Calamity, Druvenlode is the primary source of precious metal in Western Wynandir.

name: Rexxentrum
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 205,200

Rexxentrum is the majestic capital of the Dwendalian Empire and the largest city in Wildemount. It is the cultural and military center of Western Wynandir.

name: Odessloe
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 6,970

Adjacent to the clear waters of the Erdeloch, Odessloe is a prominent fishing and logging town, supplying Rexxentrum and the surrounding townships with fresh fish and lumber.

name: Nogvurot
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 15,270

A major stop along the Glory Run Road heading eastward from the capital, Nogvurot hosts travelers making their way through the empire.

name: Shadycreek Run
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 14,770

Originally comprised of a patchwork of makeshift, impoverished societies run by cruel slumlords, Shadycreek Run is now known as a hive of criminal activity and savage humanity given free reign.

name: Boroftkrah
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 3,060

The residents of the remote settlement of Boroftkrah value deeds done in service to the Boroftkrah orc clan. A council of elder warriors governs the town.

name: Palebank Village
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 690

Along the cold shore of the Frigid Depths, Palebank Village marks the first surviving Uthodurn outpost beyond the mountains.

name: Allowak's Sanctuary
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 532

Oops, all Yetis.

name: Balenpost
description:
Outpost · Wildemount · Population 205

The inhabitants of Balenpost are dedicated to planning and executing expeditions to uncover Aeorian items on the island.

name: Syrinlya
description:
Outpost · Wildemount · Population 182

Syrinlya is a collection of yurts filled with mercenary explorers from Uthodurn looking to get rich.

name: Uthodurn
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 26,240

Hidden among the icy peaks of the Flotket Alps, the dwarven stronghold of Uthodurn is a bastion of dwarven culture. The hardy folks of the city have endured the freezing temperatures and continuous snowfall for centuries.

name: Bysaes Tyl
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 19,090

The rustic elven city of Bysaes Tyl is built synergistically within the tangled boreal forests.

name: Icehaven
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 5,090

Icehaven is home to the dozen or so ships in the region outfitted to wander the ice floes of the Frigid Depths. The city is a central trading post for hunters and adventurers who brave the Saltwallow Bog or the frozen north toward Eiselcross.

name: Yrrosa
description:
Town · Wildemount · Population 3,220

Comprised of huble homesteads and numerous inns, the town of Yrrosa is a comfortable stop among the surrounding frigid fields.

name: Blumenthal
description:
Town · Wildemount · Population 3,850

A sprawling collection of rural farmsteads connected by administration buildings, Blumenthal is Rexxentrum's main source of produce and meat.

name: Othe
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 8,320

Othe is the smallest of the coastal Clovis Concord domains. Often left to their own devices, the denizens of Othe are quite reserved and are less hospitable to outsiders who don't go out of their way to engage with the locals.

name: Feolinn
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 12,700

Commonly referred to as the Garden City for its rural aesthetic, Feolinn acts as a harbor for the plentiful fishing around the Mother's Sigh Reef.

name: Port Zoon
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 19,120

Port Zoon is the industrial center of the Clovis Concord. What the city lacks in liveliness and splendor, it makes up for in manufacturing and cutting-edge science.

name: Nicodranas
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 31,900

The easternmost city of the Menagerie Coast, Nicodranas acts as an embassy state between the Clovis Concord and Dwendalian Empire.

name: Brokenbank
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 1,520

Despite its important role in the business of the coast, Brokenback lacks the strong cultural identity of the mainland city-states. The settlement is not recognized as an official city by the concord.

name: Darktow
description:
Town · Wildemount · Population 1,306

The eerie, fog-shrouded town of Darktow was originally established as a sanctuary for sailors traveling to Wildemount from Marquet or Tal'Dorei. The town is now the center of piracy in the Lucidian Ocean.

name: Talonstadt
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 1,810

Home to refugees of the ravenite resistance, the settlement of Talonstadt is not viewed favorably in the eyes of the Crown. Crime, monsters, and the residents themselves trouble the community in this shantytown.

name: Asarius
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 48,025

Known as the City of Beasts, Asarius is a cluttered sprawl of roughshod homesteads, mud huts, and military fortifications. The city is a cultural hub for the wilder folk of the Kryn Dynasty.

name: Rosohna
description:
City · Wildemount · Population 113,190

Built on the remains of Ghor Dranas, Rosohna is the center of the powerful Kryn society. A network of caves and tunnels make up an unseen level of the city beneath the surface.

name: Tomb of the Worm
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 345

A clan of wildfolk found the exposed body of Quajath the Undermaw poking through the wall of an icy cave. The people consumed the exposed meat and found that it regrew. They invite others to partake and are fully under the sway of the Undermaw.

name: Vurmas
description:
Settlement · Wildemount · Population 193

The floating outpost of Vurmas is made of three sailing ships tied together and is governed by the Kryn Dynasty. The settlement is kept secret and was established to make allies of Eiselcross's wildfolk.

name: Whitestone
description:
City · Tal'Dorei · Population 4,230

Founded by the de Rolo family, the city of Whitestone gets its name from the miraculous mineral that is the city's chief export.

name: Lyrengorn
description:
City · Tal'Dorei · Population 5,430

Meaning "mountain city" in elvish, Lyrengorn is the last bastion of safety before the endless
ice of the Neverfields to the north.

name: Terrah
description:
Village · Tal'Dorei · Population 673

Sheltered within a cauldron-like valley miles deep into the northern mountain range, the village of Terrah is home to the Ashari people that watch over the rift to the Elemental Plane of Earth.

name: Kraghammer
description:
City · Tal'Dorei · Population 43,550

Heralded as the true living center of dwarven culture
on the continent, the stronghold of Kraghammer is an autonomous city-state built upon rich veins of platinum and mithral.

name: Jorenn Village
description:
Town · Tal'Dorei · Population 1,873

Silver veins in the nearby rocky hills caused a population boom for the once tiny pioneer town of Jorenn Village.

name: Westruun
description:
City · Tal'Dorei · Population 26,205

The city of Westruun is the center of Tal’Dorei, a
crossroads of culture along the Silvercut Roadway that
welcomes adventurers, traders, and vagabonds of all
kinds.

name: Turst Fields
description:
Town · Tal'Dorei · Population 1,390

The small farming community of Turst Fields is a hot spot for trade caravans, as it is one of the main sources for food and produce in all of Tal'Dorei.

name: Drynna
description:
Town · Tal'Dorei · Population 2,230

Thriving on bountiful wildlife around Mooren Lake, the town of Drynna is responsible for most of the freshwater fish supply in eastern Tal'Dorei.

name: Zephrah
description:
Village · Tal'Dorei · Population 410

At the top of one of a mountain in the Summit Peaks, Zephrah is the tribal home of the Air Ashari people who keep watch over a gate into the Elemental Plane of Air.

name: Stilben
description:
City · Tal'Dorei · Population 9,015

Known as the "Rotted Lot" by passing sailors and merchants, Stilben is the largest port city on the eastern coast of Tal'Dorei and is the focal point for all trade to the East.

name: Kymal
description:
City · Tal'Dorei · Population 7,991

The city of Kymal stands as the grumbling ghost of a
gold rush mining center now become a haven for gamblers
and criminals.

name: Emon
description:
Metropolis · Tal'Dorei · Population 287,550

Emon is the cultural heart of human civilization on Tal'Dorei and is the nexus of politics, justice, business, industry, and education within the realm.

name: Emerald Outpost
description:
Town · Tal'Dorei · Population 1,456

Originally established by the elves of Syngorn as a
hidden outpost to keep watch over the growing empire
of Drassig, the Emerald Outpost now stands as a trade post between Emon and Syngorn.

name: Syngorn
description:
City · Tal'Dorei · Population 37,030

Founded by the sorceress Yenlara in the wake of the
Divergence, Syngorn marked the first major point of elven
civilization on Tal’Dorei following its calamitous destruction.

name: Bronbog
description:
Village · Tal'Dorei · Population 740

A shadow of a greater time, Bronbog is now only recognized for its access to the rare swamp fungus, queenscap.

name: Hdar-Fye
description:
City · Tal'Dorei

Hdar-Fye is the necropolis of Princ Hdar and swears fealty to the Iron Emperor in Tz'Arrm as a entity underneath the Iron Authority.

name: Ezordam-Haar
description:
City · Tal'Dorei

Known as the eyrie of the red pegasi, the city-state of Ezordam-Haar is underneath the Iron Authority.

name: Tz'Arrm
description:
City · Tal'Dorei · Population 47,400

Ruled by the Iron Emperor, the hobgoblin city of Tz'Arrm is meticulously ordered and hellishly hot.

name: Rybad-Kol
description:
City · Tal'Dorei

Rybad-Kol, the city of the Forge Lords, is a hobgoblin city under the rule of the Iron Authority.

name: Ortem-Vellak
description:
City · Tal'Dorei

Inside the trunk of a petrified elven tree, the city of Ortem-Vellak is governed under the mighty hobgoblin empire, the Iron Authority.

name: Byroden
description:
Town · Tal'Dorei · Population 850

The northernmost, and second-largest community in the Mornset Countryside, Byroden borders the Gladepools and marks the first stop for any traveler endeavoring to traverse the Rifenmist.

name: O'Noa
description:
Ruins · Tal'Dorei · Population 336

Devastated by the Scattered War, O'Noa was once a great colony for the first human visitors of Tal'Dorei. A small community of criminals, addicts, and opportunists have settled in the derelict city of what is now called New O'Noa.

name: Niirdal-Poc
description:
City · Tal'Dorei · Population 15,000

Founded as the capital of the Qoniira Tetrarchy long before the Calamity, the city of Niirdal-Poc lies hidden in the depths of the Rifenmist Jungle.

name: Sepesca
description:
Point of Interest (Ruins) · Wildemount

Established after the Calamity, Sepesca—meaning "Nature's End" to the Ki'Nau people—was established as a holy burial temple dedicated to the Wild Mother. The area was raided by goblins and the temple fell into ruin.

name: The Wuyun Gate
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Standing roughly twenty feet tall and fifteen feet wide, the Wuyun Gate opens for travelers granted passage to enter or exit the Dwendalian Empire.

name: Herethis Mines
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

A bountiful vein of precious platinum lead to the construction of the vast Herethis Mines.

name: Deepriver Mine
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Established around a plentiful source of iron and copper within a dry, subterranean riverbed, the Deepriver Mine is one of the Kryn Dynasty's most productive mining operations.

name: Ghor Veles
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Meaning "Vault of Shadows," Ghor Veles hides a vast dungeon beneath its ruins.

name: Olc Skerry
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Legends say the people of Olc Skerry were either slaughtered by outlanders or claimed by contagion. Sages suggest the inhabitants journeyed into the mainlands. Either way, explorers return from Olc Skerry with legends of pernicious phantoms.

name: Blooming Grove
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Deep in the Savalirwood, the centuries-old Blooming Grove hosts a small temple that has held back the curse that darkens the forest.

name: Molaesmyr
description:
Point of Interest (Ruins) · Wildemount

Once a metropolis filled with erudite scholars and beautiful elven architecture, the city of Molaesmyr lies in ruin under a perpetual fog.

name: Uraliss
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

The small town of Uraliss was Uthodurn's first attempt to expand beyond its mountain halls. However, the settlement was beset by orcs and was quickly abandoned.

name: Cinderrest Sanctum
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Hidden along the eastern slopes of Kravaraad is a cavern of onyx, where a pool of uncooling lava sits at the heart of the black stone grotto. Black iron adornments and supports honor the legend of Melora granting stone and ore to Moradin the All-Hammer.

name: Kravaraad
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

The volcano of Kravaraad looms in defiance against the frost and cold of the Greying Wildlands.

name: Mutalos
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

At the dawn of every day, the enivonment of Mutalos suddently changes, shifting from desert to forest to mountainous to swamp without any discernible pattern. The region is avoided by the local wildfolk at all costs.

name: Fortress of the Dead Jarl
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

From the outside, the Fortress of the Dead Jarl is an enormous fairy-tale castle made of ice. Inside the castle holds horrors of an entirely different story...

name: Aeor Crash Site
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

While pieces of Aeor were thrown all over Eiselcross, the city's main crash initially created a three-mile-diameter crater in the center of Foren.

name: Shattengrod
description:
Point of Interest (Ruins) · Wildemount

The giant sinkhole that once was Shattengrod has been excavated heavily, leaving the pit as a spiraling subterranean city with broken relics and forgotten secrets.

name: The Pallid Grove
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Once a beautiful, untouched woodland, the Pallid Grove was home to reclusive elves and protected by illusory magic. During the Calamity, the forest was drained of life and transformed into the spectral valley it is today.

name: Draconia
description:
Point of Interest (Ruins) · Wildemount

The once-proud nation of Draconia lies in ruins at the bottom of the Dreemoth Ravine. Originally the home of dragonborn society in Wildemount, Draconia was attacked by the Chroma Conclave, sundering the floating islands and killing many inhabitants.

name: The Miskath Pit
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

An arcane sinkhole of unknown depth and origin, the Miskath Pit is filled with psychedlic sights and sounds that madden and derange the few surviving explorers.

name: Incanter's Rest
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

The Calamity-era battlefield of Incanter's Rest is now a massive graveyard haunted by the undead spirts of unnameable horrors. There are few landmarks apart from the gigantic skeletal remains of three monstrous titans.

name: Dumaran
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

The fortress of Dumaran is home to hobgoblin survivors of the Calamity. Empowered by the Spider Queen that lured them to Dumaran, the hobgoblins plan to seek punishment for the Kryn for their insolence.

name: Mythburrow
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Locals tell stories of a monstrous, ancient being that lives in the Mythburrow. Tales of a bellowing roar that shakes the valley and sightings of an immense white-scaled dragon have been told for centuries.

name: Vergesson Sanatorium
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Once a vast prison known as the Archevault, the Vergesson Sanitorium is run by the crown and treats patients of physical and mental ailments. However, the pleasant mansions hides much darker secrets.

name: Rastum Den
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

The moderately sized encampment of Rastum Den is nestled in a small valley elevated in the mountains. The area's dizzying heights and tumultuous weather make it all but inaccessible to those without a griffon rider's prowess.

name: Diver's Grave
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Wildemount

Deep beneath the waves of the open Lucidian Ocean lies a terrifying graveyard of wrecked ships across the ocean floor. Each salvage ship that tries to reclaim cargo from the Diver's Grave only adds another corpse to its hungry sands.

name: Fort Daxio
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

Nestled within the Othendin Pass, Fort Daxio is a massive
stronghold and series of military structures under
the rule of the Tal’Dorei Council that trains and holds
the bulk of the mobile army of Emon.

name: Serpent's Head
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

Surrounded by ruins left by the Cinder King, Serpent's Head is a squat mountain in the southern reaches of the Cliffkeep Mountains.

name: The Pools of Wittebak
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

A once peaceful city occupied by thousands of rock gnomes, The Pools of Wittebak harbor now harbor hill giants and the sea hag, Mauvlettir in the dark and toxic caverns beneath the pools.

name: Murdoon Mines
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

An iron-rich mine at the base of the Cliffkeep Mountains.

name: Gatshadow
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

The monolithic mountain of Gatshadow stands unnaturally tall against the surrounding mountains. It was transformed into a place of evil as the seat of power for a priest of the Betrayer God.

name: Shadebarrow
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

Once a monolithic henge used as a ritual site for the hierophant druids, the Shadebarrow is now a forgotten crypt from where treasure hunters and historians seldom return.

name: Silvercut Crossroads
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

Marked by the crossing point between the Silvercut Roadway
and the Wildwood Byway, the Silvercut Crossroads
is a strange and solemn stop along those paths.

name: Throne of the Archeart
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

In the foothills of the Ironseat Ridge is a small rise known
as the Throne of the Archeart: a gathering place for the
many nomadic centaur tribes that ride across the Dividing
Plains.

name: Torthil
description:
Point of Interest (Ruins) · Tal'Dorei

Now just a scarred patch of land, Torthil played an important role in the Scattered War, harboring the ambush that led to the death of King Warren Drassig.

name: The Shifting Keep
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

Built within and around a cluster of massive, living trees that form the heart of the Expanse, the Shifting Keep acts as the satellite military compound for the Syngornian scouts and rangers that patrol and defend the surrounding forest.

name: Tormor Falls
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

Feeding the mouth of the rushing Feshun River that
carves through the body of the Expanse, Tormor Falls is an incredible multi-level series of waterfalls that cascade
down the eastern side of Orencleft Mountain for hundreds
of feet.

name: The Mirescar
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

Surrounded by myths in Syngornian folklore, the Mirescar is an extremely dense and seemingly corrupted forest that allows nearly no
sunlight to enter.

name: Wrettis
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

A relic of the Age of Arcanum, Wrettis is the ruined
tower of a powerful mage driven mad by the seductive
whispers of beings from the beyond.

name: The Ashen Gorge
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

Once home to Thordak, the Cinder King, the Ashen Gorge (otherwise known as the Dragon's Throne) stands tall with blazing twin peaks as a site of supernatural power.

name: Cavern of Axiom
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

The Cavern of Axiom is a lost shrine to the Knowing
Mistress spoken of only in riddles. Hidden by ever-shifting
illusions, the entrance to this cavern opens only to
those who are expected by fate.

name: Scar of the Cinder King
description:
Point of Interest (Landmark) · Tal'Dorei

Corrupted by the destruction done by Thordak, this area east of Emon radiates an invisible power.

name: The Emerald Gulch
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: The Frigid Depths
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: Torrid Reef
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: Mother's Sigh Reef
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: Dragshallow Reefs
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: Lucidian Ocean
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: Deep Row Reef
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: Southern Gate Reef
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: Inkclaw Reef
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: Ashkeeper Peaks
description: Region — Mountains · Wildemount

name: Penumbra Range
description: Region — Mountains · Wildemount

name: Vermaloc Wildwood
description: Region — Forest · Wildemount

name: The Brokenveil Marsh
description: Region — Swamp · Wildemount

name: Lotusden Greenwood
description: Region — Forest · Wildemount

name: Whitedawn Lagoon
description: Region — Waters · Wildemount

name: Quoraska Jungle
description: Region — Forest · Wildemount

name: Cyrios End Forest
description: Region — Forest · Wildemount

name: Plumgroves
description: Region — Forest · Wildemount

name: Cyrengreen Forest
description: Region — Forest · Wildemount

name: Cyrios Mountains
description: Region — Mountains · Wildemount

name: Pearlbow Wilderness
description: Region — Forest · Wildemount

name: Savalirwood
description: Region — Forest · Wildemount

name: Rime Plains
description: Region — Snowlands · Wildemount

name: Crystalsands Tundra
description: Region — Snowlands · Wildemount

name: Dunrock Mountains
